title_guess: Radnage / Oxford (Chiltern) weekend with David & Sally

pages.text:
The Motor Museum is my favourite sort - gloriously cluttered, an Alvis, a Morgan - a marvellous place.

We had lunch & all of us walked off to Lower Slaughter. The church is pretty with a lovely alabaster carving, Italian, Pisa, 1912 behind the altar, of the crucifixion, St. John & St. Mary. An old, 400 yr old, dove cot, much like a small house which held 1000 doves for eating (netted) in winter for pigeon pie.

We had a supper (hard work) outdoors at a pub with a playground. Charles slept alone in the tent.

Sunday

Off to church at __Lower Slaughter__, to find Tonic's tag, which had fallen off. A good service, talk on Philemon. We drove through Upper Slaughter, even prettier. Back to the camp to pack up.

Friday 2nd October

We left at 5.15. to meet William & Peter at the Oxford Services off the Malvern coach. David & Sally, [?Maid] Dylan & Thomas joined us at __Radnage__ [****] near Oxford at [*] 7.30. It's a very pretty view of Chiltern fields to the north. We ate in our

separate vans because it's so cold. Charles & Thomas were difficult. Charles was smacked. We went to bed at 9.30, & slept until 8.30.
__Saturday Oct 3d__
	After separate breakfasts we went on a super 6 mile walk along the valley & through woods. We came to a deep cleft & swing. The boys got filthy. Thomas loved racing around. Lunch at 3, then we played a mega game of foot ball. Late blackberries.
	We booked supper at 7 at the Crown Pub next door. We had lots of choice, including chicken breast & sauces & Tennessee Grasshopper Pie.
__Sunday Oct 4th__.
	Eve grabbed a bowl of cereal & set off with Thomas & Tonic to Church. Simon did breakfast with the others & then some people played cricket.
	It was a 30 minute walk to church along foot paths & roads. A short, family service with a lay-reader. Sermon on revelation. The [illegible] [?1523] of a man The [illegible]
